items.title: Updated Wolves cap situation after re-signing Ayo Dosunmu.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Updated Wolves cap situation after re-signing Ayo Dosunmu.

His $22.4 million annual salary likely means a cap space team was lurking to give him a salary above the $15 million mid-level.

They are $31 million below the first apron. Question is if they stay below that or the tax.

items.title: I got Ayo Dosunmu back in the Dunc'd On Mock Offseason for 4/$80M ($20M AAV) with @nateduncannba.bsky.social acting as the player agent.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
I got Ayo Dosunmu back in the Dunc'd On Mock Offseason for 4/$80M ($20M AAV) with @nateduncannba.bsky.social acting as the player agent.

5/$112M is $22.4M AAV. I feel pretty good about being in the ballpark there!

items.title: [Charania] Free agent guard Ayo Dosunmu intends to sign a five-year, $112 million contract to return to the Minnesota Timberwolves, with a player option in the fifth season, sources tell ESPN. Timberwolves made it a major priority to lock in Dosunmu after his tremendous postseason.

items.title: [Krawczynski] In moving Randle and 28 to Brooklyn, the Wolves also have all the spending power they need to bring back Ayo Dosunmu and open up access to the full mid-level exception.
items.author: JoeBiden2020FTW
items.body_excerpt: Interesting, it doesn't seem like anything is queued up for the Wolves. They might have just wanted the cap relief. Source
